title: Adler site registry
description: Schema for adler-core/data/sites.json. Field-level validation for editors and CI; deeper invariants (CSS-selector validity, case-insensitive duplicate names) are enforced by the Rust loader.

_comment.description: Free-text provenance/attribution note. Ignored by the loader.

name.description: Human-readable site name. Doubles as the case-insensitive --only/--exclude filter key; must be unique. Restricted character class (word chars plus space and a few punctuation marks) keeps the name safe to interpolate into shell, CSV, and CLI arguments — schema gate against injection via PR-supplied site entries.

url.description: URL template. Must start with http(s):// and contain the {username} placeholder. Prefer a stable API/feed endpoint over a bot-protected canonical page (see CONTRIBUTING.md).

signals.description: Detection signals. Aggregation is negative-priority: any NotFound vote wins over Found; no votes -> Uncertain.

known_present.description: One or more usernames known to exist on this site. Accepts either a single string (compact, legacy) or a non-empty array (defensive — `adler --doctor` passes the present-check if any element resolves to Found, guarding against sites that special-case specific accounts).

known_absent.description: A username known not to exist. Optional; the doctor generates a random nonsense username when omitted.

extract.description: Optional CSS-selector rules for --enrich profile extraction.

tags.description: Free-form classification tags for --tag subset scans (e.g. "social", "dev", "region:ru"). Conventionally lowercase.

request_headers.description: Extra HTTP headers sent with the probe (e.g. `X-IG-App-ID` for Instagram). Browser backends apply via setExtraHTTPHeaders/setUserAgentOverride; the raw-HTTP path doesn't read this yet.

regex_check.description: Username-validity regex (Rust `regex` syntax). Usernames not matching this pattern are skipped with `Uncertain(UsernameNotAllowed)` — no HTTP request is issued. Mirrors Sherlock's `regexCheck`.
